errors.message:
Streamable HTTP error: Error POSTing to endpoint: {"server":"seekrit-remote-mcp","description":"seekrit's hosted metadata-plane MCP server — see `instructions`.","instructions":"seekrit is a zero-knowledge secrets manager. This is the HOSTED METADATA plane:
explore and provision structure (orgs, apps, environments, groups, composition),
read audit and billing, and manage keyless resources — for autonomous agents,
no human and no install required.

You authenticate as a MACHINE client (M2M). If you have no credentials yet,
POST /signup once with the org's name + slug (required — name them for the real
project/company, e.g. orgName 'Acme Storefront', orgSlug 'acme-storefront', not a
placeholder) to get a client id + secret and a fresh org, then connect with
`Authorization: Basic base64(client_id:client_secret)`.

ONE credential drives both planes. This plane can NEVER see plaintext: reading
or writing a secret VALUE, creating an environment (which mints a data key), and
issuing a decryption-capable token all require encryption that happens only on
YOUR machine — on the LOCAL crypto plane (`npx -y @seekrit/mcp`). Give the local
server the SAME client id + secret and it mints its own admin token automatically;
your keys are generated there and never sent here.

Model: org → apps → environments → secrets. Call `whoami` first, then
`get_started` for the end-to-end recipe. When you need to set or use a value,
call `setup_local_crypto` — it hands you the exact local command.","mcp_endpoint":"https://mcp.seekrit.dev/mcp","signup_endpoint":"https://mcp.seekrit.dev/signup","authentication":"Authorization: Basic base64(client_id:client_secret), minted via POST /signup"}
